subject: Geography
title: Colliding Continents
description: Learn Colliding Continents in one minute.

hook.text: India is crashing into Asia at five centimeters per year—and has been for 50 million years.

buildup.text: When two continental plates collide, neither can sink. The crust crumples upward.

discovery.text: That collision built the Himalayas, and the mountains are still rising today.

twist.text: Erosion works just as hard. Rain and ice tear down what tectonics push up.

climax.text: Mountains exist in a constant battle between the forces that build and destroy them.

punchline.text: Continents collide in slow motion. Mountains are the scars.
